calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-06-2014 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-31-2013 Continued To Jan-07-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bernardo Silva, Esq. for Elizabeth Davis. Andrew Curtis, Esq. specially appearing for Allen Zimmerman, Esq. for Law Finance Group. Eleanor Lewis and Betty Higgins in Propria Persona (Beneficiaries).

The Court and counsel had a brief discussion regarding the intent of Ms. Mouton to gift Dwayne $500.00 (minus $110.00 and interested accrued for a total of $584.24), the declaration filed July 29, 2013 clarifying that the descendant had changed her mind about the gift, and the codicil not reflecting the change reported in the declaration. Mr. Curtis explained the terms of the loans taken out by Eleanor and Betty, and that his company is exempt from usury laws. The Court requests that Mr. Curtis file a declaration regarding the interest rate (to be filed by January 07, 2014). Mr. Curtis and Mr. Silva come to the agreement that future interest on the loans will not be charged from December 31, 2013 on. The accounting petition #1 was continued to January 07, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and is to be deemed submitted on that date. The Court may bifurcate the issue of the pecuniary gifts.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Nancy Bautista. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-11-2013 Continued To Dec-31-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Oct-22-2013 Continued To Nov-12-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1077 Palou, San Francisco, Ca 94124)
Off Calendar Feb-11-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bernardo Silva for Elizabeth Davis.

Mr. Silva stated that the home is on one single parcel of land. The Court clarified that it is not making a determination as to the two lots and that the sale is based on the legal descriptions as indicated in the reports of sale. The sale for 1077 and 1081 Palou Street was confirmed to Louis Caruana on behalf of Hector Quinonez at $555,000.00 subject to the terms and conditions as set forth in both reports of sale filed on October 28, 2012. The Court granted the petitions (subject to receipt and review of the orders). No additional bond is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
